series_metadata.series_description: This series contains records of employment for each ODPS employee including: documents related to hire (application/resume and supporting documents, e.g., educational transcripts/diplomas, military discharge, character/professional references, and memo of approval); employee work rule agreements and acknowledgements; personnel forms, which may include, but are not limited to, ID badge information, requests for specialty positions, applications/approvals of secondary employment, Fair Labor Standards Act (FLSA) and essential employee designations, changes of address, and PERS form A; completion of service letters; personnel actions (not including disciplinary actions with the exception of termination); letters of commendation and recognition; emergency contact information; and exit interviews. Note: The following personnel records are retained separately: Backgrounds (background checks) and polygraphs conducted for employment in accordance with SAN 760-0788 and SAN 760-0773; Employee medical and benefits records in accordance with SAN 760-1443 (non-OSHP/OIU employees) and SAN 760-1072 (OSHP/OIU employees); Employee training records in accordance with SAN 760-1538; Employee performance evaluations in accordance with SAN 760-1437; I-9 Employee Verification Forms in accordance with SAN 760-1532; and Employee disciplinary records in accordance with SAN 760-0787.

retention_rules.disposition: Paper: Retain for life of service, plus 50 years from employee''''''''s date of separation. Record may be retained electronically. If record is imaged and retained electronically, paper may be destroyed as soon as scan is confirmed and validated. ; Electronic: Personnel Actions are retained in the Electronic Personnel Action Request (EPAR) system. Remaining records, if retained electronically, are retained in HR''''''''s Intellinetics System, PeopleSoft, and ERIS. Retain for life of service, plus 50 years from employee''''''''s date of separation., Paper: Shred ; Electronic: Delete
